data.title: Beiersdorf: Weaker Q1, Questionable Guidance but Limited Downside Keeps Rating at Hold
data.description:
Jefferies analyst David Hayes has maintained their neutral stance on 0DQ7 stock, giving a Hold rating on April 15.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hayes has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Beiersdorf’s recent performance and guidance. First‑quarter organic sales came in weaker than already reduced expectations, and the sharp deterioration in March suggests trading conditions were significantly tougher than management had anticipated, raising questions about the reliability of near‑term forecasting.

At the same time, management has reiterated its full‑year sales and operating margin targets, which Hayes views as optimistic given the soft momentum of the Nivea franchise and the likelihood that margins may need to be structurally reset. However, these concerns are partially balanced by the share price trading around a 10‑year low, which limits downside in the near term and supports a neutral, rather than outright negative, stance on the stock.

In another report released on April 15, Kepler Capital also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €85.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Diagnostyka S.A.: Strong Revenue Momentum but Margin Headwinds Justify Neutral ‘Hold’ Rating
data.description:
James Vane, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Hold rating on Diagnostyka S.A.. The associated price target is PLN188.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

James Vane has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Diagnostyka S.A.’s recent performance and outlook. The company delivered strong top-line growth in FY25, with revenues rising faster than expected and driven by both higher volumes and price increases, yet profitability lagged as operating costs grew more quickly, resulting in EBITDA that fell short of market expectations and a notable margin decline versus prior guidance.

At the same time, management’s forward-looking indications point to continued revenue expansion in the low- to mid-teens percent range and modest volume growth, but only a partial recovery in margins, which are now projected merely to be close to current levels rather than improve toward earlier forecasts. Combined with planned capex, a moderate leverage profile and the need for further clarity on margin pressures and growth trajectories from the upcoming conference call, these mixed signals support a more neutral stance rather than a clear buy or sell recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: THG: Sustained Revenue Momentum and Beauty-Led Growth Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Andrew Wade maintained a Buy rating on THG today and set a price target of p60.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Wade has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors highlighting THG’s improving fundamentals and consistent execution. He points to another solid quarter of growth in both Beauty and Nutrition, aligned with the trajectory previously outlined, and notes that management has reaffirmed guidance, supporting his view of around 30% EBITDA growth with healthy cash generation for the year.

He also emphasizes that THG has now delivered four consecutive quarters of positive revenue momentum, even after adjusting for disposals and discontinued operations, underscoring a sustainable recovery in the top line. Within Beauty, he cites strong performances from Lookfantastic in the U.K. and Dermstore in the U.S., including customer base expansion and market outperformance, which together reinforce his confidence in the group’s strategic positioning and justify the Buy recommendation.

Wade covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Greggs plc, THG, and Pets at Home. According to TipRanks, Wade has an average return of 3.7% and a 53.25% success rate on recommended stocks.
